title: The Preterite (Pretérito Indefinido), Deepened
description: Go beyond regular -AR verbs: the full -ER/-IR endings, the high-frequency irregulars (ser/ir, tener, hacer, estar, poder...), spelling changes (llegué, busqué, empecé) and the -IR stem-changers (pidió, durmió).

cards.front: hablé, hablaste, habló, hablamos, hablasteis, hablaron
cards.notes: Regular **-AR** endings: **-é, -aste, -ó, -amos, -asteis, -aron**. Note the accents on *hablé* and *habló* — they change the meaning (*hablo* = I speak; *habló* = he/she spoke).

cards.front: comí, comiste, comió, comimos, comisteis, comieron
cards.back: preterite of -ER and -IR verbs (comer = to eat)
cards.notes: **-ER** and **-IR** verbs share one set of endings: **-í, -iste, -ió, -imos, -isteis, -ieron**. So *comer* and *vivir* are conjugated the same way in the preterite: *comí / viví*.

cards.front: fui, fuiste, fue, fuimos, fuisteis, fueron
cards.notes: *ser* (to be) and *ir* (to go) have the **same** preterite. *Fui médico* = I was a doctor; *Fui a casa* = I went home. Context tells them apart.

cards.front: tuve, tuviste, tuvo, tuvimos, tuvisteis, tuvieron
cards.notes: *tener* uses the irregular stem **tuv-** plus the special endings **-e, -iste, -o, -imos, -isteis, -ieron** (no accents, and *tuvo* not *tuvió*). These same endings are shared by many irregulars.

cards.front: hice, hiciste, hizo, hicimos, hicisteis, hicieron
cards.back: preterite of hacer (to do / to make)
cards.notes: Irregular stem **hic-**, but watch the spelling: *él/ella* form is **hizo** (with z) to keep the soft sound. *¿Qué hiciste ayer?* = What did you do yesterday?

cards.back: more irregular stems: estar, poder, poner, venir, querer
cards.notes: Same special endings, different stems: *estar* → **estuv-**, *poder* → **pud-**, *poner* → **pus-**, *venir* → **vin-**, *querer* → **quis-**. E.g. *Estuve en Madrid* = I was in Madrid.

cards.front: dije, dijiste, dijo, dijimos, dijisteis, dijeron
cards.back: preterite of decir (to say / to tell)
cards.notes: Stem **dij-**. After a **j**, the *ellos* ending drops the *i*: **dijeron** (not *dijieron*). The same happens with *traer* → *trajeron* and *conducir* → *condujeron*.

cards.back: spelling changes in the yo form (-gar, -car, -zar)
cards.notes: To keep the sound, the **yo** form changes spelling: *llegar* → **llegué**, *buscar* → **busqué**, *empezar* → **empecé**. Only the *yo* form changes; the rest are regular (*llegaste, llegó*...).
Stem-changing **-IR** verbs change the vowel only in the *él/ella* and *ellos* forms: *pedir* → **pidió/pidieron**, *dormir* → **durmió/durmieron**. The other forms are regular (*pedí, pediste*).

cards.front: ayer, anoche, la semana pasada, en 2019
cards.back: time markers that trigger the preterite
cards.notes: The preterite reports **completed** actions at a definite past time. Words like *ayer* (yesterday), *anoche* (last night), *el año pasado* (last year) and specific dates signal it.

cards.back: no 'did' helper — the verb itself carries the past
cards.notes: English uses *did* for the past (*I did eat / I ate*). Spanish has **no** *did* helper — the ending alone marks the past: *comí* = I ate. Never translate *did* as *hice*.

cards.front: vi, viste, vio, vimos, visteis, vieron
cards.back: preterite of ver (to see) — no accents
cards.notes: *ver* is regular but its short forms take **no** written accent: **vi**, **vio** (not *ví, vió*). *Lo vi ayer* = I saw it/him yesterday.

steps.title: One past tense, fully formed
steps.body:
# The Preterite, deepened

In A1 you met the **pretérito indefinido** (the preterite) for completed past actions, mostly with **-AR** verbs. Now we complete the picture.

First, the two regular patterns:

| | -AR (*hablar*) | -ER/-IR (*comer / vivir*) |
|---|---|---|
| yo | hablé | comí |
| tú | hablaste | comiste |
| él/ella | habló | comió |
| nosotros | hablamos | comimos |
| vosotros | hablasteis | comisteis |
| ellos | hablaron | comieron |

**-ER and -IR verbs share one set of endings.** Note the accented *yo* and *él* forms: *hablé / habló*, *comí / comió*.

> ⚠️ **No "did".** English builds the past with a helper (*Did you eat? I did not eat*). Spanish does **not** — the ending alone carries the past: *¿Comiste? No comí.* There is no word for *did*.

steps.title: The irregular stems
steps.body:
## High-frequency irregulars

A group of common verbs uses a **new stem** plus one shared set of endings — and crucially these endings have **no accents**:

> **-e, -iste, -o, -imos, -isteis, -ieron**

| Verb | Stem | yo / él |
|---|---|---|
| tener | tuv- | tuve / tuvo |
| estar | estuv- | estuve / estuvo |
| poder | pud- | pude / pudo |
| poner | pus- | puse / puso |
| venir | vin- | vine / vino |
| querer | quis- | quise / quiso |
| hacer | hic- | hice / **hizo** |

Note *hizo* with a **z** (spelling, to keep the soft sound).

**ser and ir are identical** in the preterite: *fui, fuiste, fue, fuimos, fuisteis, fueron*. *Fui a Perú* (I went) vs *Fui feliz* (I was happy) — context decides.

After a **j-stem** (*decir* → *dij-*, *traer* → *traj-*), the *ellos* ending loses its *i*: **dijeron**, **trajeron** (not *dijieron*).

steps.title: Spelling changes and -IR stem-changers
steps.body:
## Two small but important patterns

**1. Yo-form spelling (-car, -gar, -zar).** To preserve the consonant sound, the *yo* form changes — and only the *yo* form:

- *buscar* → **busqué** (c → qu)
- *llegar* → **llegué** (g → gu)
- *empezar* → **empecé** (z → c)

The rest stay regular: *busqué, buscaste, buscó...*

**2. -IR stem-changers in the 3rd person.** Stem-changing **-IR** verbs (and only -IR) shift their vowel in the *él/ella* and *ellos* forms:

- *pedir* → pedí, pediste, **pidió**, pedimos, pedisteis, **pidieron** (e → i)
- *dormir* → dormí, dormiste, **durmió**, ..., **durmieron** (o → u)

Also common: *preferir* → prefirió, *servir* → sirvió, *morir* → murió, *seguir* → siguió.

exercise.prompt: Match each subject with the correct preterite form of *comer* (to eat).
Translate: *She went to Mexico last year.*
accept: Fue a México el año pasado.
fue a méxico el año pasado
Fue a Mexico el ano pasado.
Ella fue a México el año pasado.
Fue a México el año pasado
exercise.hint: ir → fue (él/ella). 'last year' = el año pasado.
distractors: Fui a México el año pasado.
Ella va a México el año pasado.
Fuió a México el año pasado.

exercise.prompt: A friend asks what you did. Which sentence means 'I did not eat anything'? (Remember: no 'did' helper.)
exercise.hint: The ending of *comer* already carries the past — no extra verb for 'did'.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the él/ella preterite of *hacer*. Mind the spelling!
exercise.hint: hacer → hic-, but the él form is spelled with z: hizo.

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words: *I had a problem yesterday.*
Translate: *I arrived late.* (llegar — careful with the yo form)
Fill in the él/ella preterite of *pedir* (to ask for / order).
exercise.sentence: El camarero trajo lo que ella ___.
exercise.hint: pedir is an -IR stem-changer: e → i in the él form → pidió.
decir → dij-; after j the ellos ending drops the i → dijeron.

exercise.prompt: Match each verb with its irregular preterite stem.
Translate: *I was in Madrid last week.* (use estar)
accept: Estuve en Madrid la semana pasada.
estuve en madrid la semana pasada
Estuve en Madrid la semana pasada
exercise.hint: estar → estuve (yo). 'last week' = la semana pasada.
distractors: Fui en Madrid la semana pasada.
Estuvé en Madrid la semana pasada.
Estaba en Madrid la semana pasada.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *We slept well and they slept badly.* (dormir)
accept: Nosotros dormimos bien y ellos durmieron mal.
exercise.hint: nosotros is regular (dormimos); ellos stem-changes o→u (durmieron).
